devdoc.author: Kerne Protocol
devdoc.details: FLOW:      1. Protocol fees (USDC, WETH, etc.) accumulate in this contract.      2. The buyback bot (or anyone) calls `executeBuyback()` to swap fees for KERNE.      3. Purchased KERNE is distributed to the staking contract as rewards.      This creates a self-reinforcing loop: more TVL → more fees → more KERNE buybacks      → higher KERNE price → more attractive staking APY → more TVL.

InsufficientBalance().details: Thrown when the treasury lacks sufficient token balance for an operation.

KerneTokenNotSet().details: Thrown when the KERNE token address has not been configured.

NoLiquidityForBuyback().details: Thrown when a buyback preview returns zero (no liquidity available).

OwnableInvalidOwner(address).details: The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)

OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address).details: The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.

SafeERC20FailedOperation(address).details: An operation with an ERC-20 token failed.

SlippageExceeded().details: Thrown when actual swap output is less than the minimum acceptable (slippage protection).

SlippageTooHigh().details: Thrown when the slippage tolerance exceeds the maximum allowed (10%).

StakingContractNotSet().details: Thrown when the staking contract address has not been configured.

TokenNotApprovedForBuyback().details: Thrown when a token is not approved for buyback operations.

ZeroAddress().details: Thrown when a zero address is provided where a valid address is required.

ZeroAmount().details: Thrown when an amount is zero but must be positive.

params._aerodromeRouter: The Aerodrome router for executing swaps.
params._stakingContract: The staking contract that receives purchased KERNE.

emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256,addres.details: Only callable by owner (Kerne multisig). Use for emergency recovery of mis-sent tokens      or urgent rebalancing. This bypasses the normal buyback flow — use with care.      No event is emitted intentionally; on-chain tx calldata provides the audit trail.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to withdraw.
params.recipient: The address to send the tokens to (must be non-zero).
params.token: The ERC-20 token to withdraw from the treasury.

executeBuyback(address,uint256).details: Swaps `amount` of `token` for KERNE via Aerodrome, then distributes      the purchased KERNE to the staking contract as rewards.      If a routing hop is set for `token`, the swap routes through it:      e.g. USDC → WETH → KERNE (better liquidity than direct USDC/KERNE).

params.amount: The amount of fee tokens to use in this buyback.
params.token: The fee token to sell for KERNE.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

returns.minOut: The minimum acceptable output after slippage.

receiveFees(address,uint256).details: Any approved token can be sent here. The treasury accumulates them      until a buyback cycle is triggered.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setRouter(address).details: Only callable by owner. Only needed if Aerodrome deploys a new router version.      After updating, verify that the new router supports the same route format before      triggering any buyback calls to avoid failed swaps.

params._router: The new Aerodrome router contract address.

setRoutingHop(address,address).details: Use this when there's no direct liquidity between `token` and KERNE.      e.g. setRoutingHop(USDC, WETH) routes USDC → WETH → KERNE.

params.hop: The intermediate token to route through.

setStakingContract(address).details: Only callable by owner. The new staking contract must be able to handle      incoming KERNE transfers as reward distributions. Coordinate with the staking      contract team before changing this address to avoid disrupting reward flows.

sweepETH(address,uint256).details: SECURITY (audit 2026-05-11): without this, ETH sent to the treasury      via `receive()`, selfdestruct injection, or a misrouted refund had      no exit path — `emergencyWithdraw` only handles ERC-20 tokens. ETH      could accumulate indefinitely with no way to recover. Owner-only;      recipient must be non-zero. Uses `.call` (not `.transfer`) to      support smart-wallet recipients like Safe.

params.amount: The amount of ETH (wei) to sweep.
params.recipient: The address to receive the ETH.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

updateKerneToken(address).details: Only callable by owner. Rare operation — only needed if KERNE token migrates to a new contract.      All subsequent buybacks will target the new token address. Ensure the new token has      sufficient Aerodrome liquidity before updating, or existing buyback calls will fail.

params._kerneToken: The new KERNE token contract address.

devdoc.title: KerneTreasury

BuybackExecuted(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Emitted when a KERNE buyback swap is executed successfully.

BuybackTokenApproved(address,bool).notice: Emitted when a token is approved or revoked for use in KERNE buybacks.

FeeReceived(address,uint256,address).notice: Emitted when fee tokens are received from protocol components.

KerneDistributed(address,uint256).notice: Emitted when purchased KERNE is distributed to the staking contract as rewards.

KerneTokenUpdated(address,address).notice: Emitted when the KERNE token address is updated (rare migration event).

RoutingHopSet(address,address).notice: Emitted when a routing hop is configured for a fee token (multi-hop swap path).

SlippageUpdated(uint256,uint256).notice: Emitted when the buyback slippage tolerance is updated.

StakingContractUpdated(address,address).notice: Emitted when the staking contract address is updated.

_previewSwap(address,address,uint256).notice: Internal preview helper — external so it can be called with try/catch.

aerodromeRouter().notice: The Aerodrome router — executes all KERNE buyback swaps.

approvedBuybackTokens(address).notice: Tokens approved for use in KERNE buybacks (e.g. USDC, WETH).

emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256,addres.notice: Emergency withdrawal of any token held in the treasury to a recipient address.

executeBuyback(address,uint256).notice: Executes a KERNE buyback using accumulated fee tokens.

kerneToken().notice: The KERNE governance token — the target of all buybacks.

previewBuyback(address,uint256).notice: Previews the expected KERNE output for a given buyback.

receiveFees(address,uint256).notice: Accepts incoming fee tokens from protocol components.

routingHops(address).notice: Optional routing hops for multi-hop swaps (e.g. USDC → WETH → KERNE).         If set for a token, the buyback routes through this intermediate token.

setApprovedBuybackToken(address,bool).notice: Approves or revokes a token for use in KERNE buybacks.

setRouter(address).notice: Updates the Aerodrome router address used for buyback swaps.

setRoutingHop(address,address).notice: Sets a routing hop for multi-hop buyback swaps.

setSlippage(uint256).notice: Updates the slippage tolerance for buyback swaps.

setStakingContract(address).notice: Updates the staking contract that receives purchased KERNE rewards.

slippageBps().notice: Slippage tolerance for buyback swaps in basis points (default: 200 = 2%).         Protects against sandwich attacks while allowing reasonable price impact.

stakingContract().notice: The staking contract — receives purchased KERNE as rewards.

sweepETH(address,uint256).notice: Sweeps native ETH out of the treasury to a recipient.

userdoc.notice: Protocol treasury that accumulates fee revenue and executes KERNE buybacks via Aerodrome.         This is the terminal destination for all protocol performance fees — it converts them         into KERNE buy pressure, feeding the BribeVortex flywheel and the staking rewards pool.
